fix: contract save crash - missing DB columns + update whitelist

Root cause: penalty_per_day_pct / max_penalty_pct were in TypeScript model
but not in the database, causing UPDATE to throw 'column does not exist'.

- Migration 124: ADD COLUMN penalty_per_day_pct, max_penalty_pct to contracts
- contracts.api.ts create: add penalty fields to INSERT payload
- contracts.api.ts list/create: map penalty fields from DB rows
- contracts.api.ts update: replace blind .update(input) with explicit
  whitelist to prevent unknown columns from crashing any future save

fix: test-cov mise task silently drops CLI args; profile test wrong on 3.14 (#331)

* fix: add usage field to test-cov task and fix testing profile test for Python 3.14

The test-cov mise task silently dropped -m markers because it had no
usage field defined. CI marker expressions like 'not dev_only' were
never applied, causing dev_only/docker/performance tests to run and
fail in CI.

Also fix test_testing_profile_uses_ultralight_constants to account for
HAS_FASTEMBED=False on Python 3.14, where sparse embedding uses
ibm-granite/granite-embedding-30m-sparse instead of qdrant/bm25.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/knitli/codeweaver/sessions/4b9a7c93-cd45-4002-95fa-66e63b8b6981

* fix: eliminate eval in test-cov, parametrize profile test over HAS_FASTEMBED

Address review feedback:
- Replace eval+string-building with POSIX-safe `set --` argument list
  so each arg is passed as its own token (no shell injection risk)
- Parametrize test_testing_profile_uses_ultralight_constants over
  HAS_FASTEMBED=True/False so both backend paths are validated
  deterministically in every test run
- Fix: ST branch reranking assertion now correctly expects
  ULTRALIGHT_ST_RERANKING_MODEL (was asserting fastembed constant)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/knitli/codeweaver/sessions/435f7a0d-d419-41c7-a426-7b93ef59a126

---------

Signed-off-by: Adam Poulemanos <89049923+bashandbone@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Phase 1 Rust workspace — 5 crates, MCP server, ProcessSandbox

Implements the complete Phase 1 backend:

Crates:
- sandcastle-runtime: SandboxRuntime trait, types (SandboxId, ExecRequest,
  ExecResult, SandboxConfig, ResourceLimits), error types with MCP error codes
- sandcastle-executor: In-sandbox binary, reads JSON from stdin, spawns
  language runtime (python3/node/bash), captures output with timeout/OOM
- sandcastle-manager: Session registry, file validation (allowed_input_dirs,
  path traversal), reaper for expired sessions, oneshot + session execution
- sandcastle-process: ProcessSandbox using libcontainer (youki), OCI spec
  generation via oci-spec crate, namespace isolation (pid/mount/ipc/uts/net),
  pipe-based executor communication, workspace bind-mounts
- sandcastle-server: MCP server using rmcp crate, 6 tools (execute_code,
  create_sandbox, execute_in_session, upload_file, download_file,
  destroy_sandbox), stdio transport, reaper background task

Tests:
- 15 unit tests (9 runtime types, 5 manager with mock runtime, 1 OCI spec)
- Integration test script: executor direct test, timeout handling, MCP server
  tool listing

Tooling:
- scripts/build-rootfs.sh: Docker export rootfs builder for python/bash/node

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 14: Shimmer Animation (#33)

## Summary

**Phase 14: Shimmer Animation**
**Goal:** Users see a smooth animated skeleton shimmer while translation
is in progress, with no jarring layout shifts
**Status:** Verified ✓ (3/3 must-haves passed)

Adds a polished left-to-right gradient sweep shimmer animation to the
popup loading state. The shimmer plays from translation start until the
result appears, using `.plusLighter` blend mode that adapts to both
light and dark system appearance. Implemented as a zero-layout-impact
`.overlay` ViewModifier to avoid interfering with the popup's
GeometryReader height measurement. Fully respects the Reduce Motion
accessibility setting.

## Changes

### Plan 14-01: ShimmerModifier ViewModifier + Unit Tests
Left-to-right gradient sweep ViewModifier using `.plusLighter` blend
mode with Reduce Motion accessibility fallback.

**Key files created:**
- `Transy/Popup/ShimmerModifier.swift` — ViewModifier with animated
gradient sweep, `.clipped()`, `.allowsHitTesting(false)`
- `TransyTests/ShimmerModifierTests.swift` — 3 structural tests

### Plan 14-02: Wire Shimmer into LoadingPopupText
Connected `ShimmerModifier` to the actual loading UI via `.shimmer()` on
`PopupText` inside `LoadingPopupText.body`.

**Key files modified:**
- `Transy/Popup/PopupView.swift` — Added `.shimmer()` call on
loading-state text

## Requirements Addressed

| ID | Requirement | Status |
|----|------------|--------|
| SHM-01 | Shimmer animation plays on popup text during translation
loading | ✓ |
| SHM-02 | Zero layout impact — no resize notification storm | ✓ |
| SHM-03 | Reduce Motion disables animation — static muted text fallback
| ✓ |

## Verification

- [x] Automated verification: PASSED (3/3 must-haves)
- [x] Human visual verification: gradient sweep, no layout shift, Reduce
Motion compliance
- [x] Unit tests: all pass (`make test`)

## Key Decisions

- `.overlay` architecture (not ZStack) to prevent GeometryReader height
measurement interference
- `.plusLighter` blend mode for light/dark adaptive shimmer appearance
- 1.5s linear `repeatForever(autoreverses: false)` sweep speed
- `.onAppear` for animation start (not `.task`) per pitfall analysis

Closes #28

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace agent-framework metapackage with agent-framework-core

The agent-framework metapackage pulls agent-framework-core[all], which
installs 25+ unused provider packages (pythonnet, boto3, anthropic,
claude-sdk, etc). These cause intermittent ImportError on CI due to
namespace package conflicts in the agent_framework namespace.

Replace with agent-framework-core directly — we only need core, foundry,
openai, and ollama providers. Reduces dependencies from 167 to 81 packages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(macos): fix clipboard copy failing from tray and GUI (#4629)

The clipboard copy from tray menu and capture GUI was broken on macOS:

1. FlameshotDaemon::copyToClipboard() created a KDSingleApplication
   instance and called isPrimaryInstance(), which always returned false
   (the daemon already holds the socket). This caused a 3-second IPC
   timeout before falling back. Fix: use instance() to check if the
   daemon singleton exists in-process, matching non-macOS behavior.

2. saveToClipboardMime() used setData("image/...") which puts a lazy
   reference on the macOS pasteboard. If the app exits before paste,
   data is lost ("Cannot keep promise"). Fix: use setImageData() for
   native pasteboard persistence, plus setData() for exact format.

3. saveJpegToClipboardMacOS() used osascript with a temp file and
   incorrectly labeled JPEG data as PNG UTI. Removed entirely — the
   fixed saveToClipboardMime() now handles both JPEG and PNG.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Feat(blueprint): Add read-before-write check

Before calling guesty.set_custom_field, read the current
value via guesty.get_custom_field_values and compare it to
the slot_code. Skip the write when the values already match.
This avoids unnecessary API calls on every periodic sync
and reduces Guesty API usage.

commit.message:
feat(dev-tools): converge dev runtime into unified 09-dev-tools layer (#47)

* feat(dev-tools): converge dev runtime into unified 09-dev-tools layer

Create the canonical dev-tools module (src/dev-tools/) that establishes a
single authoritative development-tooling layer for file watching, rebuild
triggering, hot reload orchestration, and lifecycle events.

New components:
- FileWatcher: canonical file watcher with debouncing, filtering, batch
  merging, and path management (extracted from dev-session inline chokidar)
- HotReloadOrchestrator: wraps HotReloadClient with before/after hooks,
  sequence tracking, and concurrent reload protection
- DevServer: unified dev runtime facade composing FileWatcher +
  BuildPipeline + HotReloadOrchestrator with full state machine and event
  system

Architecture:
- DevServer is the new canonical dev API (createDevServer)
- DevSession refactored to compose the same dev-tools primitives
  (FileWatcher, HotReloadOrchestrator) for backward compatibility
- Both paths share the same unified orchestration components
- executeDev/dev-command continues to work through DevSession unchanged

Tests:
- 10 FileWatcher tests (FW-01 through FW-10)
- 12 HotReloadOrchestrator tests (HR-01 through HR-12)
- 15 DevServer tests (DV-01 through DV-15)
- All 29 existing DevSession tests pass unmodified
- All 3 existing dev-command tests pass unmodified
- Full suite: 1513 pass, 0 fail, 1 skip

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(dev-tools): address foundation review feedback

* refactor(dev-tools): make DevSession a thin DevServer wrapper

commit.message:
macb: Circle-matched init, RX descriptor diag, BAR2 firmware preserve

- MACB init rewritten to match Circle's macb.cpp sequence:
  - DMACFG read-modify-write preserving firmware defaults
  - RXBMS=3 (max), TXPBMS=1, FBLDO=16
  - TX addr=0 during init (set during send, Circle pattern)
  - NCR: TE|RE only, MPE toggled per MDIO operation
  - Minimal NCFGR with DBW from DCFG1 before PHY, full after negotiation
- MACB RX: volatile raw pointer read + DSB/ISB for DMA visibility
  - Periodic descriptor dump (raw[0], raw[1], RSR, RBQP)
  - Clears RSR to track new BNA events
- PCIe: preserve firmware BAR2 config if already programmed
  - Log firmware BAR2_LO/HI/REMAP before any modification
  - Only program BAR2 from scratch if size field is zero
- Runtime: RXCNT confirms frames arrive at MAC but BNA persists

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add 'Open app' intent link from sourcePackage field

When a notification includes sourcePackage (e.g. com.google.android.deskclock),
a small pill-shaped '📲 Open app' link is shown below the body. The href uses
the Android intent:// URI scheme which Chrome on Android follows to launch the
originating app directly:
  intent://#Intent;package=<pkg>;action=android.intent.action.MAIN;
          category=android.intent.category.LAUNCHER;end

On desktop browsers the link does nothing harmful (no app to open).
This covers the missing intent on the 'Klocka' (Clock) notification.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: advanced tutorial notebooks, @algo API, EPEX/validation fixes — release 0.4.0b1 (#14)

* Add advanced tutorial notebooks: @algo API and custom signals

Add two new Jupyter notebooks covering the advanced concepts not
addressed by the existing walkthroughs:

- notebooks/algo_decorator_walkthrough.ipynb
  Covers the @algo async event-stream API: event type hierarchy,
  building a DA price logger, an IDC spread scalper (step-by-step),
  gate closure management, and signals via ctx.get_signal().
  Includes visualisations of the event timeline and backtest PnL.

- notebooks/custom_signals_walkthrough.ipynb
  Covers implementing the SignalProvider protocol: DataFrameSignalProvider
  from scratch, publication_offset semantics with a timeline chart,
  signal history lookback, get_delivery_position(), and gate closure
  NOP analysis.

Also updates README.md to replace the single notebook link with a table
of all four notebooks in suggested reading order.

All four notebooks execute end-to-end without errors (make execute-notebooks).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: IDC VWAP benchmarking, GateClosureWarning, and equity curve accuracy

- Compute per-product IDC market VWAPs incrementally from HistoricalTrade
  events (O(P) memory, compatible with sliding-window replay); each fill
  is now benchmarked against its own product's market VWAP rather than a
  single portfolio-level number, giving accurate win/loss attribution
- Emit GateClosureWarning to the @algo event stream one MTU before gate
  closure for each IDC product, giving the algo a last chance to act
- Equity curve snapshots now use running per-product VWAPs instead of
  zero, making the intra-run curve directionally meaningful
- Extract _dispatch_signals() helper to eliminate duplicated signal loop
  between DA and IDC paths
- Update notebooks and tests to match

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Bump _version.py to 0.4.0b1
- Update README implementation status table to mark @algo, EPEX SPOT,
  gate closure NOP tracking, and validation pipeline as Done
